title: NPM authentication secrets should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: NPM _auth credential detected in .npmrc format. The _auth parameter in .npmrc files contains base64-encoded username:password credentials for basic authentication to npm registries. Exposing these credentials allows attackers to authenticate as you, publish malicious packages, access private packages, and compromise your npm account. Use npm login or modern access tokens (_authToken) instead of basic authentication.

NPM _password credential detected in .npmrc format. The _password parameter in .npmrc files stores plaintext passwords for basic authentication to npm registries. Exposing these passwords enables attackers to gain full access to your npm account, publish malicious packages, access private packages, and compromise the software supply chain. Use npm login or modern _authToken authentication instead.

NPM username/password variable detected in configuration. Variables containing NPM username and password combinations provide basic authentication credentials to npm registries. Exposing these credentials allows unauthorized package publishing, access to private registries, account takeover, and supply chain attacks. Use npm login or modern authentication tokens instead of hardcoded credentials.

NPM Basic Auth credential detected - username:password format. NPM basic authentication credentials in username:password format provide direct registry access. Exposing these credentials enables attackers to authenticate as you, publish packages with your identity, access private packages, modify package metadata, and launch supply chain attacks. Use npm login or modern access tokens instead of basic authentication.
